title: Scaling Tokenization for Multilingual Ad Queries
summary: How we built a language-adaptive tokenizer that handles 40+ languages at billion-query scale with sub-millisecond latency.
content:
At Microsoft Bing Ads, one of the core challenges in query understanding is tokenization — breaking raw user queries into meaningful units that downstream models can consume. Standard approaches like WordPiece or BPE work well for English but struggle with morphologically rich languages (Turkish, Finnish) and unsegmented scripts (Chinese, Japanese, Thai).

We developed a hybrid tokenizer that combines a shared multilingual vocabulary with language-specific fallback strategies. The key insight is that for advertising queries, which are typically short (3–7 words), we can afford slightly richer tokenization without blowing up latency budgets. Our system detects the query language using a lightweight n-gram classifier, then selects the appropriate tokenization path.

The architecture uses a two-tier approach: a fast BPE pass for high-resource languages and a character-level fallback with learned composition for tail languages. We pre-compute token-to-embedding mappings for the top 2M tokens, keeping inference under 0.5ms at p99.

Early results show a 3.2% improvement in ad relevance metrics for non-English markets, with negligible latency regression. The system currently processes ~15B queries/day across all markets.
